## Definition
The driver is matching the expert reference closely — line, brake
points, throttle application, apex speed are all within tolerance.
There's no mistake to label and no recovery to make.

## When this applies
The default for clean segments — no specific mistake fires and the
time-difference-to-expert is flat or closing.

## How to read clean segments in a session
A clean lap is the goal. A high proportion of clean segments across a
session means the driver is consistent and on pace. A driver with 80%
clean and 20% mistake segments is a faster driver to coach than one
who is 50/50 — focus on the few mistakes rather than rebuilding
fundamentals.

## Engineer interpretation
Nothing to fix here. If the driver asks "how was that lap?" and most
of the segments are clean, the honest answer is "clean — that's the
benchmark, do it again." Don't invent feedback when there isn't any.
The trap is filling silence with generic advice; resist it.
